- claude-mem:do: Execute a phased implementation plan using subagents. Use when asked to execute, run, or carry out a plan — especially one created by make-plan.
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- cloudflare:durable-objects: Create and review Cloudflare Durable Objects. Use when building stateful coordination (chat rooms, multiplayer games, booking systems), implementing RPC methods, SQLite storage, alarms, WebSockets, or reviewing DO code for best practices. Covers Workers integration, wrangler config, and testing with Vitest. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- cloudflare:wrangler: Cloudflare Workers CLI for deploying, developing, and managing Workers, KV, R2, D1, Vectorize, Hyperdrive, Workers AI, Containers, Queues, Workflows, Pipelines, and Secrets Store. Load before running wrangler commands to ensure correct syntax and best practices. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- cloudflare:cloudflare-email-service: Send and receive transactional emails with Cloudflare Email Service (Email Sending + Email Routing). Use when building email sending (Workers binding or REST API), email routing, Agents SDK email handling, or integrating email into any app — Workers, Node.js, Python, Go, etc. Also use for email deliverability, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, wrangler email setup, MCP email tools, or when a coding agent needs to send emails. Even for simple requests like "add email to my Worker" — this skill has critical config details.
- cloudflare:workers-best-practices: Reviews and authors Cloudflare Workers code against production best practices. Load when writing new Workers, reviewing Worker code, configuring wrangler.jsonc, or checking for common Workers anti-patterns (streaming, floating promises, global state, secrets, bindings, observability). Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- cloudflare:sandbox-sdk: Build sandboxed applications for secure code execution. Load when building AI code execution, code interpreters, CI/CD systems, interactive dev environments, or executing untrusted code. Covers Sandbox SDK lifecycle, commands, files, code interpreter, and preview URLs. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- cloudflare:cloudflare: Comprehensive Cloudflare platform skill covering Workers, Pages, storage (KV, D1, R2), AI (Workers AI, Vectorize, Agents SDK), feature flags (Flagship), networking (Tunnel, Spectrum), security (WAF, DDoS), and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi). Use for any Cloudflare development task. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- cloudflare:agents-sdk: Build AI agents on Cloudflare Workers using the Agents SDK. Load when creating stateful agents, durable workflows, real-time WebSocket apps, scheduled tasks, MCP servers, chat applications, voice agents, or browser automation. Covers Agent class, state management, callable RPC, Workflows, durable execution, queues, retries, observability, and React hooks. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- cloudflare:web-perf: Analyzes web performance using Chrome DevTools MCP. Measures Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) and supplementary metrics (FCP, TBT, Speed Index), identifies render-blocking resources, network dependency chains, layout shifts, caching issues, and accessibility gaps. Use when asked to audit, profile, debug, or optimize page load performance, Lighthouse scores, or site speed. Biases towards retrieval from current documentation over pre-trained knowledge.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines.       3. **Show me details** - Deep dive into specific issues

      Choose [1], [2], or specify which issue to examine:</ask>

    <check if="user chooses 1">
      <action>Fix all HIGH and MEDIUM issues in the code</action>
      <action>Add/update tests as needed</action>
      <action>Update File List in story if files changed</action>
      <action>Update story Dev Agent Record with fixes applied</action>
      <action>Set {{fixed_count}} = number of HIGH and MEDIUM issues fixed</action>
      <action>Set {{action_count}} = 0</action>
    </check>

    <check if="user chooses 2">
      <action>Add "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection to Tasks/Subtasks</action>
      <action>For each issue: `- [ ] [AI-Review][Severity] Description [file:line]`</action>
      <action>Set {{action_count}} = number of action items created</action>
      <action>Set {{fixed_count}} = 0</action>
    </check>

    <check if="user chooses 3">
      <action>Show detailed explanation with code examples</action>
      <action>Return to fix decision</action>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="5" goal="Update story status and sync sprint tracking">
    <!-- Determine new status based on CRITICAL issues only (v3.0) -->
    <!-- HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW are tracked as action items but don't block automation -->
    <check if="0 CRITICAL issues remain after fixes">
      <action>Set {{new_status}} = "done"</action>
      <action>Update story Status field to "done"</action>
    </check>
    <check if="1+ CRITICAL issues remain after fixes">
      <action>Set {{new_status}} = "in-progress"</action>
      <action>Update story Status field to "in-progress"</action>
    </check>
    <action>Save story file</action>

    <!-- Determine sprint tracking status -->
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists">
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "enabled"</action>
    </check>
    <check if="{sprint_status} file does NOT exist">
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "no-sprint-tracking"</action>
    </check>

    <!-- Sync sprint-status.yaml when story status changes (only if sprint tracking enabled) -->
    <check if="{{current_sprint_status}} != 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {sprint_status}</action>
      <action>Find development_status key matching {{story_key}}</action>

      <check if="{{new_status}} == 'done'">
        <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "done"</action>
        <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure</action>
        <output>✅ Sprint status synced: {{story_key}} → done</output>
      </check>

      <check if="{{new_status}} == 'in-progress'">
        <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "in-progress"</action>
        <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure</action>
        <output>🔄 Sprint status synced: {{story_key}} → in-progress</output>
      </check>

      <check if="story key not found in sprint status">
        <output>⚠️ Story file updated, but sprint-status sync failed: {{story_key}} not found in sprint-status.yaml</output>
      </check>
    </check>

    <check if="{{current_sprint_status}} == 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <output>ℹ️ Story status updated (no sprint tracking configured)</output>
    </check>

    <output>**✅ Review Complete!**

      **Story Status:** {{new_status}}
      **Issues Fixed:** {{fixed_count}}
      **Action Items Created:** {{action_count}}

      {{#if new_status == "done"}}Code review complete!{{else}}Address the action items and continue development.{{/if}}
    </output>
  </step>

</workflow>

5	# Story 2.7: Version pinning for deepwiki
6	
7	Status: review
8	
9	<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
10	
11	## Story
12	
13	As a developer targeting a specific library version,
14	I want to run `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin <version>` to generate a skill for that exact version,
15	so that my wiki skill reflects a known release rather than latest HEAD.
16	
17	## Acceptance Criteria
18	
19	1. **Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin v2.0.0` where `v2.0.0` is a valid semver tag on the repo, **When** the pipeline runs, **Then** AN scopes against the pinned version's code, **And** CS records the pinned version in metadata.json provenance, **And** the generated skill reflects the v2.0.0 API surface.
20	
21	2. **Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin v99.0.0` where `v99.0.0` does not exist, **When** the pipeline starts, **Then** the pipeline halts with a clear error listing the invalid pin, **And** the error includes suggested corrections (e.g., available tags matching `v99.*`).
22	
23	3. **Given** `deepwiki <repo-url>` without `--pin`, **When** the pipeline runs, **Then** the latest release tag is used as the default pin, **And** metadata.json records the resolved version.
24	
25	4. **Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin main` with a branch name instead of a tag, **When** the pipeline runs, **Then** the branch name is accepted as a valid pin, **And** AN scopes against the branch HEAD.
26	
27	5. **Given** pin validation logic, **When** comparing with campaign pin validation (FR-32), **Then** both use the same shared validation module (no duplicate implementation).
28	
29	## Tasks / Subtasks
30	
31	- [x] Task 1: Create shared pin validation script `skf-validate-pins.py` (AC: #2, #4, #5)
32	  - [x] 1.1 Create `src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-pins.py` following the existing shared script pattern (argparse, JSON stdout, stderr errors, exit codes 0/1/2).
33	  - [x] 1.2 Accept args: `--repo-url <url>` (required), `--pin <version>` (optional — when absent, resolve latest release tag), `--format tag|branch|any` (optional, default `any`).
34	  - [x] 1.3 Implement tag resolution: list tags via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/tags --paginate --jq '.[].name'` with fallback to `git ls-remote --tags`. Match against: exact, `v`-prefixed, `{name}@{version}`, `{name}/v{version}`, `{name}/{version}`, `{name}-v{version}`.
35	  - [x] 1.4 Implement branch resolution: `git ls-remote --heads "{repo_url}" "{pin}"` — verify branch exists.
36	  - [x] 1.5 Implement latest-release resolution (no --pin): `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/latest --jq '.tag_name'` with fallback to `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/tags --jq '.[0].name'`.
37	  - [x] 1.6 Output JSON: `{"status": "valid"|"invalid"|"resolved", "pin": "<input>", "resolved_ref": "<tag_or_branch>", "ref_type": "tag"|"branch", "version": "<semver_or_null>", "suggestions": ["<nearest_tags>"]}`. Exit 0 = valid/resolved, 1 = invalid (no match), 2 = error.
38	  - [x] 1.7 On invalid pin, populate `suggestions` with up to 5 nearest tags (matching prefix pattern from the pin, e.g., `v99.*` → list tags starting with `v99.` or nearest by semver sort).
39	
40	- [x] Task 2: Create test file for pin validation (AC: #2, #4, #5)
41	  - [x] 2.1 Create `test/test-skf-validate-pins.py` following the existing test pattern.
42	  - [x] 2.2 Test cases: valid semver tag, valid v-prefixed tag, valid branch name, invalid pin with suggestions, latest-release resolution (no --pin), error handling (unreachable repo).
43	  - [x] 2.3 Register the test file in `package.json` `test:python` script.
44	
45	- [x] Task 3: Add §0b Pin Resolution to step-auto-scope.md (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
46	  - [x] 3.1 Insert new §0b "Pin Resolution" between §0 (URL Type Detection) and §0c (Coexistence Detection). §0b runs only for repo URLs and local paths — skip for documentation URLs (doc URLs have no git repo to pin against).
47	  - [x] 3.2 §0b reads the `--pin` argument from the pipeline context (the `[auto]` pipeline passes `--pin` alongside `--project-path`).
48	  - [x] 3.3 **When `--pin` is provided:** invoke `skf-validate-pins.py --repo-url {project_path} --pin {pin_value}`. Handle exit codes:
49	    - Exit 0 (`status: "valid"`): store `{pinned_ref}` = `resolved_ref`, `{pinned_ref_type}` = `ref_type`, `{pinned_version}` = `version`. Continue to §0c.
50	    - Exit 1 (`status: "invalid"`): HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit error: `"Version pin '{pin_value}' not found in {project_path}. Available matches: {suggestions}. Use a valid tag, branch, or omit --pin for latest."` Emit error envelope with `halt_reason: "pin-invalid"`.
51	    - Exit 2 (error): HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit error envelope with `halt_reason: "resolution-failure"`.
52	  - [x] 3.4 **When `--pin` is NOT provided (default):** invoke `skf-validate-pins.py --repo-url {project_path}` (no --pin flag). Handle exit codes:
53	    - Exit 0 (`status: "resolved"`): store `{pinned_ref}` = `resolved_ref`, `{pinned_ref_type}` = `ref_type`, `{pinned_version}` = `version`. Log: "Default pin resolved: {resolved_ref}". Continue to §0c.
54	    - Exit 1 (no releases found): set `{pinned_ref}` = null, `{pinned_ref_type}` = null, `{pinned_version}` = null. Log: "No release tags found — using HEAD." Continue to §0c without pinning.
55	    - Exit 2: log warning, continue without pinning (same as exit 1 behavior).
56	  - [x] 3.5 For local paths: when `--pin` is provided, emit a warning: "**Local source may not match pinned version {pin_value}.** Ensure you've checked out the correct version locally, or use a remote GitHub URL so SKF can clone from the git tag automatically." Store `{pinned_ref}` = `{pin_value}`, `{pinned_ref_type}` = "local", `{pinned_version}` = `{pin_value}`. Continue to §0c without running `skf-validate-pins.py` (can't validate tags on a local path).
57	
58	- [x] Task 4: Pass pin data to brief write in §8 (AC: #1, #3)
59	  - [x] 4.1 In §8 (Write Skill Brief), when `{pinned_ref}` is non-null:
60	    - If `{pinned_ref_type}` is `"tag"`: set `target_version` = `{pinned_version}` (the semver extracted from the tag), set `target_ref` = `{pinned_ref}` (the git tag name), set `version` = `{pinned_version}`.
61	    - If `{pinned_ref_type}` is `"branch"`: set `target_ref` = `{pinned_ref}` (the branch name), leave `target_version` = null (branches aren't semver), `version` = auto-detected or `1.0.0`.
62	  - [x] 4.2 When `{pinned_ref}` is null (no pin, no releases): leave `target_version` = null, `target_ref` = null — existing version detection at §8 applies unchanged.
63	  - [x] 4.3 In the docs-only path (§0a), `--pin` is ignored (already skipped at §0b). No changes needed to §0a.
64	
65	- [x] Task 5: Pass pin data to brief write in multi-scope §4a/§6a (AC: #1)
66	  - [x] 5.1 In §4a/§6a (multi-scope decomposition), apply the same `target_version`/`target_ref` values from §0b to all N decomposed briefs. All boundaries of a decomposed repo share the same pin — the pin targets a repo-level ref, not a package-level version.
67	
68	- [x] Task 6: Update AN SKILL.md documentation (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
69	  - [x] 6.1 In the Invocation Contract table, add `--pin <version>` as a new headless input: "Pin to a specific version tag or branch. Accepts semver tags, git tags, and branch names. When absent, resolves to the latest release tag."
70	  - [x] 6.2 In the Stages table, add a note to step 1a: "Includes §0b pin resolution — validates and resolves version pin before scoping"
71	  - [x] 6.3 In the Auto mode path description, add: "After URL type detection (§0), pin resolution (§0b) validates the --pin argument (or resolves the latest release tag) and stores the resolved ref for downstream brief writes."
72	  - [x] 6.4 In the Exit Codes table, document: exit code 3 includes `halt_reason: "pin-invalid"` when the supplied pin does not match any tag or branch.
73	  - [x] 6.5 In the Result Contract section, document the new `pinned_ref` and `pinned_version` fields in the envelope (present when a pin was resolved).
74	
75	- [x] Task 7: Add pin fields to the result envelope in §9 (AC: #1, #3)
76	  - [x] 7.1 When `{pinned_ref}` is non-null, include `"pinned_ref": "{pinned_ref}"` and `"pinned_version": "{pinned_version}"` in the `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` envelope. These flow downstream to BS/CS for provenance recording.
77	  - [x] 7.2 When `{pinned_ref}` is null, omit these fields from the envelope (backward-compatible — existing consumers don't expect them).
78	
79	- [x] Task 8: Verify backward compatibility (AC: #3)
80	  - [x] 8.1 Verify that when `--pin` is not provided and no releases exist, the pipeline falls back to HEAD gracefully (same behavior as today).
81	  - [x] 8.2 Verify that the `forge`, `forge-quick`, `onboard`, and `maintain` pipeline aliases are unaffected (they don't pass `[auto]`, so step-auto-scope.md is never loaded).
82	  - [x] 8.3 Verify that the interactive AN path (no `[auto]`) is unchanged — §0b is only executed when `{auto_mode}` is true.
83	  - [x] 8.4 Run `npm test` — full regression suite passes.
84	
85	## Dev Notes
86	
87	### Architecture: Pin Resolution as a Pre-Flight Gate in Auto-Scope
88	
89	Story 2.7 adds pin resolution as §0b in step-auto-scope.md — between URL type detection (§0) and coexistence detection (§0c). The change is a **validation gate**: if `--pin` is provided, the pin must resolve to a valid tag or branch before proceeding; if not provided, the latest release tag is auto-resolved as the default pin.
90	
91	**Current flow (Story 2.6):**
92	```
93	§0 URL Type Detection
94	§0c Coexistence Detection
95	  ├── doc URL → §0a Docs-Only Short-Circuit
96	  └── repo URL → §1 → §2 → ... → §11
97	```
98	
99	**New flow (Story 2.7):**
100	```
101	§0 URL Type Detection
102	§0b Pin Resolution (repo URLs and local paths only; skip for doc URLs)
103	  ├── --pin provided + valid → store ref, continue
104	  ├── --pin provided + invalid → HARD HALT (exit 3, pin-invalid)
105	  └── no --pin → resolve latest release tag, continue (fallback to no pin if none)
106	§0c Coexistence Detection
107	  (then §0a docs-only or §1 repo-based)
108	```
109	
110	§0b is a gate, not a branch — it stores the resolved pin in workflow context variables (`{pinned_ref}`, `{pinned_ref_type}`, `{pinned_version}`) that are consumed later in §8 when writing the skill brief. It does not change the flow after it (coexistence, scope, brief write), only enriches the brief with version data.
111	
112	[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Pipeline Integration — "**`--pin` flag:** Consumed by AN's first step. Passed downstream to CS (for provenance pinning) and recorded in metadata.json."]
113	
114	### How Pin Data Flows Downstream
115	
116	The pin resolution in §0b sets three workflow context variables. These are consumed at brief write time (§8 for single-scope, §4a/§6a for multi-scope). The brief's `target_version` and `target_ref` fields then drive CS's existing source resolution protocols — no changes needed to CS.
117	
118	```
119	AN §0b (pin resolution)
120	  → stores {pinned_ref}, {pinned_ref_type}, {pinned_version}
121	  
122	AN §8 (brief write)
123	  → writes target_version, target_ref to skill-brief.yaml
124	  
125	BS (brief-skill)
126	  → version-resolution.md: target_version wins over auto-detected version (existing precedence)
127	  
128	CS (create-skill)
129	  → source-resolution-protocols.md: tag resolution uses target_version/target_ref (existing logic)
130	  → compile.md §4: records source_ref in metadata.json (existing)
131	```
132	
133	No modifications needed to BS, CS, TS, or EX — the existing brief schema, version resolution, and source resolution protocols handle pinned versions natively. Story 2.7 only needs to populate the correct fields at AN's brief write.
134	
135	[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/version-resolution.md — "target_version wins outright" precedence rule]
136	[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/source-resolution-protocols.md — "Explicit Tag Resolution (when target_version is set)"]
137	[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/compile.md:100-102 — "Set `source_ref` from resolved source ref"]
138	
139	### Pin Validation vs Existing Tag Resolution
140	
141	The existing tag resolution in `source-resolution-protocols.md` has two modes:
142	- **Explicit** (target_version set): resolves to tag, warns and falls back to HEAD on failure
143	- **Implicit** (only version set): resolves to tag, warns and falls back to HEAD on failure
144	
145	Story 2.7's pin validation is **stricter** — it's a hard gate:
146	- `--pin` provided + tag/branch not found → HARD HALT (the user explicitly requested a version that doesn't exist)
147	- No `--pin` → resolve latest release, fall back to no pin gracefully (user didn't express intent)
148	
149	This strictness is intentional: `--pin` expresses explicit user intent (analogous to `target_version`), so failure to resolve is an error. Default pin resolution (no `--pin`) is opportunistic, so failure is acceptable.
150	
151	The shared `skf-validate-pins.py` script handles both the hard validation (deepwiki --pin) and the campaign pin validation (Story 4.4). Campaign will import or invoke the same script.
152	
153	### Shared Pin Validation Script Interface
154	
155	The script follows the existing shared script pattern (`skf-preflight.py`, `skf-detect-docs.py`, `skf-shape-detect.py`):
156	
157	**File:** `src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-pins.py`
158	
159	```
160	Args: --repo-url <url> [--pin <version>] [--format tag|branch|any]
161	Stdout: JSON object (see below)
162	Exit: 0 (valid/resolved), 1 (invalid — no match), 2 (error)
163	```
164	
165	**Output schema:**
166	```json
167	{
168	  "status": "valid | invalid | resolved",
169	  "pin": "<input_pin_or_null>",
170	  "resolved_ref": "<matched_tag_or_branch>",
171	  "ref_type": "tag | branch",
172	  "version": "<semver_extracted_from_tag_or_null>",
173	  "suggestions": ["<nearest_tags_on_invalid>"]
174	}
175	```
176	
177	**Behavior matrix:**
178	
179	| Input | Resolution | Status | Exit |
180	|-------|-----------|--------|------|
181	| `--pin v2.0.0` (exists as tag) | Tag match | `valid` | 0 |
182	| `--pin main` (exists as branch) | Branch match | `valid` | 0 |
183	| `--pin v99.0.0` (not found) | No match | `invalid` | 1 |
184	| No `--pin` (latest release exists) | Latest release tag | `resolved` | 0 |
185	| No `--pin` (no releases) | No tag found | `invalid` | 1 |
186	| Unreachable repo | Error | stderr JSON | 2 |
187	
188	**Tag matching priority** (same as `source-resolution-protocols.md`):
189	1. Exact match: `{pin}`
190	2. With `v` prefix: `v{pin}`
191	3. Package scope (monorepos): `{name}@{pin}`, `@{scope}/{name}@{pin}`
192	4. Crate/package prefix: `{name}/v{pin}`, `{name}/{pin}`, `{name}-v{pin}`
193	
194	**Version extraction from tag:** Strip `v` prefix if present. If the result is valid semver, set `version`. Otherwise set `version` to null (branch names, non-semver tags).
195	
196	**Suggestion generation on invalid pin:** List available tags, sort by semver descending, filter to tags sharing a prefix with the pin (e.g., `v99.*` → tags starting with `v99.`). If no prefix matches, return the 5 most recent tags. Cap suggestions at 5.
197	
198	[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Shared Script Patterns]
199	
200	### §0b Placement Rationale
201	
202	§0b (Pin Resolution) sits between §0 (URL Type Detection) and §0c (Coexistence Detection) because:
203	
204	1. **After §0:** Pin resolution needs to know the input type. Doc URLs skip pinning (no repo to pin against). Local paths get a warning. Only remote repo URLs do full validation.
205	2. **Before §0c:** Coexistence detection checks existing skills by repo URL/name. Pin doesn't affect which skills match — a pinned `v2.0.0` of `foo` still conflicts with an existing `foo` skill. So §0c runs after pin resolution unchanged.
206	3. **Before §1:** Shape detection in §1→§3 runs against the source. When a pin is resolved, shape detection should conceptually scope against the pinned ref (though the actual clone/API access happens at CS time — AN's shape detection uses the manifest at HEAD since it doesn't clone the repo).
207	
208	**Note on shape detection timing:** AN auto-scope does NOT clone the repo — it reads manifests via `gh api` or from a local path. Shape detection at §3 reads the manifest at the default branch (HEAD), not at the pinned ref. This is acceptable because:
209	- Shape classification (library-API, reference-app, etc.) is structural, not version-specific
210	- Export counts may differ between versions, but decomposition thresholds are coarse (`>500` exports, `>3` packages) and unlikely to change between versions
211	- The actual extraction against the pinned ref happens at CS time via `source-resolution-protocols.md`
212	
213	If a future version requires shape detection at the pinned ref, the solution is to pass `{pinned_ref}` as a `?ref=` parameter to `gh api` calls in §2 — a localized change, not a flow restructure.
214	
215	### Brief Field Mapping by Ref Type
216	
217	| Ref Type | `target_version` | `target_ref` | `version` | Effect in CS |
218	|----------|-----------------|-------------|-----------|-------------|
219	| Tag (semver) | `{pinned_version}` | `{pinned_ref}` | `{pinned_version}` | Explicit tag resolution → clone at tag |
220	| Branch | null | `{pinned_ref}` | auto-detect or `1.0.0` | Explicit ref override → clone at branch HEAD |
221	| No pin, latest resolved | `{pinned_version}` | `{pinned_ref}` | `{pinned_version}` | Same as tag — latest release is a tag |
222	| No pin, no releases | null | null | auto-detect or `1.0.0` | No pin → implicit resolution or HEAD |
223	
224	When `target_ref` is set, CS's `source-resolution-protocols.md` uses it verbatim (the "Explicit ref override" path) — `git ls-remote` confirms it resolves, then clones at that ref. When `target_version` is set, the "Explicit Tag Resolution" path runs. Both are existing CS behaviors; Story 2.7 only needs to set the correct brief fields.
225

227	[Source: src/skf-analyze-source/assets/skill-brief-schema.md — target_version, target_ref field definitions]

# Story 2.7: Version pinning for deepwiki

Status: review

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a developer targeting a specific library version,
I want to run `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin <version>` to generate a skill for that exact version,
so that my wiki skill reflects a known release rather than latest HEAD.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin v2.0.0` where `v2.0.0` is a valid semver tag on the repo, **When** the pipeline runs, **Then** AN scopes against the pinned version's code, **And** CS records the pinned version in metadata.json provenance, **And** the generated skill reflects the v2.0.0 API surface.

2. **Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin v99.0.0` where `v99.0.0` does not exist, **When** the pipeline starts, **Then** the pipeline halts with a clear error listing the invalid pin, **And** the error includes suggested corrections (e.g., available tags matching `v99.*`).

3. **Given** `deepwiki <repo-url>` without `--pin`, **When** the pipeline runs, **Then** the latest release tag is used as the default pin, **And** metadata.json records the resolved version.

4. **Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin main` with a branch name instead of a tag, **When** the pipeline runs, **Then** the branch name is accepted as a valid pin, **And** AN scopes against the branch HEAD.

5. **Given** pin validation logic, **When** comparing with campaign pin validation (FR-32), **Then** both use the same shared validation module (no duplicate implementation).

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create shared pin validation script `skf-validate-pins.py` (AC: #2, #4, #5)
  - [x] 1.1 Create `src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-pins.py` following the existing shared script pattern (argparse, JSON stdout, stderr errors, exit codes 0/1/2).
  - [x] 1.2 Accept args: `--repo-url <url>` (required), `--pin <version>` (optional — when absent, resolve latest release tag), `--format tag|branch|any` (optional, default `any`).
  - [x] 1.3 Implement tag resolution: list tags via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/tags --paginate --jq '.[].name'` with fallback to `git ls-remote --tags`. Match against: exact, `v`-prefixed, `{name}@{version}`, `{name}/v{version}`, `{name}/{version}`, `{name}-v{version}`.
  - [x] 1.4 Implement branch resolution: `git ls-remote --heads "{repo_url}" "{pin}"` — verify branch exists.
  - [x] 1.5 Implement latest-release resolution (no --pin): `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/latest --jq '.tag_name'` with fallback to `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/tags --jq '.[0].name'`.
  - [x] 1.6 Output JSON: `{"status": "valid"|"invalid"|"resolved", "pin": "<input>", "resolved_ref": "<tag_or_branch>", "ref_type": "tag"|"branch", "version": "<semver_or_null>", "suggestions": ["<nearest_tags>"]}`. Exit 0 = valid/resolved, 1 = invalid (no match), 2 = error.
  - [x] 1.7 On invalid pin, populate `suggestions` with up to 5 nearest tags (matching prefix pattern from the pin, e.g., `v99.*` → list tags starting with `v99.` or nearest by semver sort).

- [x] Task 2: Create test file for pin validation (AC: #2, #4, #5)
  - [x] 2.1 Create `test/test-skf-validate-pins.py` following the existing test pattern.
  - [x] 2.2 Test cases: valid semver tag, valid v-prefixed tag, valid branch name, invalid pin with suggestions, latest-release resolution (no --pin), error handling (unreachable repo).
  - [x] 2.3 Register the test file in `package.json` `test:python` script.

- [x] Task 3: Add §0b Pin Resolution to step-auto-scope.md (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 3.1 Insert new §0b "Pin Resolution" between §0 (URL Type Detection) and §0c (Coexistence Detection). §0b runs only for repo URLs and local paths — skip for documentation URLs (doc URLs have no git repo to pin against).
  - [x] 3.2 §0b reads the `--pin` argument from the pipeline context (the `[auto]` pipeline passes `--pin` alongside `--project-path`).
  - [x] 3.3 **When `--pin` is provided:** invoke `skf-validate-pins.py --repo-url {project_path} --pin {pin_value}`. Handle exit codes:
    - Exit 0 (`status: "valid"`): store `{pinned_ref}` = `resolved_ref`, `{pinned_ref_type}` = `ref_type`, `{pinned_version}` = `version`. Continue to §0c.
    - Exit 1 (`status: "invalid"`): HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit error: `"Version pin '{pin_value}' not found in {project_path}. Available matches: {suggestions}. Use a valid tag, branch, or omit --pin for latest."` Emit error envelope with `halt_reason: "pin-invalid"`.
    - Exit 2 (error): HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit error envelope with `halt_reason: "resolution-failure"`.
  - [x] 3.4 **When `--pin` is NOT provided (default):** invoke `skf-validate-pins.py --repo-url {project_path}` (no --pin flag). Handle exit codes:
    - Exit 0 (`status: "resolved"`): store `{pinned_ref}` = `resolved_ref`, `{pinned_ref_type}` = `ref_type`, `{pinned_version}` = `version`. Log: "Default pin resolved: {resolved_ref}". Continue to §0c.
    - Exit 1 (no releases found): set `{pinned_ref}` = null, `{pinned_ref_type}` = null, `{pinned_version}` = null. Log: "No release tags found — using HEAD." Continue to §0c without pinning.
    - Exit 2: log warning, continue without pinning (same as exit 1 behavior).
  - [x] 3.5 For local paths: when `--pin` is provided, emit a warning: "**Local source may not match pinned version {pin_value}.** Ensure you've checked out the correct version locally, or use a remote GitHub URL so SKF can clone from the git tag automatically." Store `{pinned_ref}` = `{pin_value}`, `{pinned_ref_type}` = "local", `{pinned_version}` = `{pin_value}`. Continue to §0c without running `skf-validate-pins.py` (can't validate tags on a local path).

- [x] Task 4: Pass pin data to brief write in §8 (AC: #1, #3)
  - [x] 4.1 In §8 (Write Skill Brief), when `{pinned_ref}` is non-null:
    - If `{pinned_ref_type}` is `"tag"`: set `target_version` = `{pinned_version}` (the semver extracted from the tag), set `target_ref` = `{pinned_ref}` (the git tag name), set `version` = `{pinned_version}`.
    - If `{pinned_ref_type}` is `"branch"`: set `target_ref` = `{pinned_ref}` (the branch name), leave `target_version` = null (branches aren't semver), `version` = auto-detected or `1.0.0`.
  - [x] 4.2 When `{pinned_ref}` is null (no pin, no releases): leave `target_version` = null, `target_ref` = null — existing version detection at §8 applies unchanged.
  - [x] 4.3 In the docs-only path (§0a), `--pin` is ignored (already skipped at §0b). No changes needed to §0a.

- [x] Task 5: Pass pin data to brief write in multi-scope §4a/§6a (AC: #1)
  - [x] 5.1 In §4a/§6a (multi-scope decomposition), apply the same `target_version`/`target_ref` values from §0b to all N decomposed briefs. All boundaries of a decomposed repo share the same pin — the pin targets a repo-level ref, not a package-level version.

- [x] Task 6: Update AN SKILL.md documentation (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 6.1 In the Invocation Contract table, add `--pin <version>` as a new headless input: "Pin to a specific version tag or branch. Accepts semver tags, git tags, and branch names. When absent, resolves to the latest release tag."
  - [x] 6.2 In the Stages table, add a note to step 1a: "Includes §0b pin resolution — validates and resolves version pin before scoping"
  - [x] 6.3 In the Auto mode path description, add: "After URL type detection (§0), pin resolution (§0b) validates the --pin argument (or resolves the latest release tag) and stores the resolved ref for downstream brief writes."
  - [x] 6.4 In the Exit Codes table, document: exit code 3 includes `halt_reason: "pin-invalid"` when the supplied pin does not match any tag or branch.
  - [x] 6.5 In the Result Contract section, document the new `pinned_ref` and `pinned_version` fields in the envelope (present when a pin was resolved).

- [x] Task 7: Add pin fields to the result envelope in §9 (AC: #1, #3)
  - [x] 7.1 When `{pinned_ref}` is non-null, include `"pinned_ref": "{pinned_ref}"` and `"pinned_version": "{pinned_version}"` in the `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` envelope. These flow downstream to BS/CS for provenance recording.

## Dev Notes

### Architecture: Pin Resolution as a Pre-Flight Gate in Auto-Scope

Story 2.7 adds pin resolution as §0b in step-auto-scope.md — between URL type detection (§0) and coexistence detection (§0c). The change is a **validation gate**: if `--pin` is provided, the pin must resolve to a valid tag or branch before proceeding; if not provided, the latest release tag is auto-resolved as the default pin.

**Current flow (Story 2.6):**
```
§0 URL Type Detection
§0c Coexistence Detection
  ├── doc URL → §0a Docs-Only Short-Circuit
  └── repo URL → §1 → §2 → ... → §11
```

**New flow (Story 2.7):**
```
§0 URL Type Detection
§0b Pin Resolution (repo URLs and local paths only; skip for doc URLs)
  ├── --pin provided + valid → store ref, continue
  ├── --pin provided + invalid → HARD HALT (exit 3, pin-invalid)
  └── no --pin → resolve latest release tag, continue (fallback to no pin if none)
§0c Coexistence Detection
  (then §0a docs-only or §1 repo-based)
```

§0b is a gate, not a branch — it stores the resolved pin in workflow context variables (`{pinned_ref}`, `{pinned_ref_type}`, `{pinned_version}`) that are consumed later in §8 when writing the skill brief. It does not change the flow after it (coexistence, scope, brief write), only enriches the brief with version data.

[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Pipeline Integration — "**`--pin` flag:** Consumed by AN's first step. Passed downstream to CS (for provenance pinning) and recorded in metadata.json."]

### How Pin Data Flows Downstream

The pin resolution in §0b sets three workflow context variables. These are consumed at brief write time (§8 for single-scope, §4a/§6a for multi-scope). The brief's `target_version` and `target_ref` fields then drive CS's existing source resolution protocols — no changes needed to CS.

```
AN §0b (pin resolution)
  → stores {pinned_ref}, {pinned_ref_type}, {pinned_version}
  
AN §8 (brief write)
  → writes target_version, target_ref to skill-brief.yaml
  
BS (brief-skill)
  → version-resolution.md: target_version wins over auto-detected version (existing precedence)
  
CS (create-skill)
  → source-resolution-protocols.md: tag resolution uses target_version/target_ref (existing logic)
  → compile.md §4: records source_ref in metadata.json (existing)
```

No modifications needed to BS, CS, TS, or EX — the existing brief schema, version resolution, and source resolution protocols handle pinned versions natively. Story 2.7 only needs to populate the correct fields at AN's brief write.

[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/version-resolution.md — "target_version wins outright" precedence rule]
[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/source-resolution-protocols.md — "Explicit Tag Resolution (when target_version is set)"]
[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/compile.md:100-102 — "Set `source_ref` from resolved source ref"]

### Pin Validation vs Existing Tag Resolution

The existing tag resolution in `source-resolution-protocols.md` has two modes:
- **Explicit** (target_version set): resolves to tag, warns and falls back to HEAD on failure
- **Implicit** (only version set): resolves to tag, warns and falls back to HEAD on failure

Story 2.7's pin validation is **stricter** — it's a hard gate:
- `--pin` provided + tag/branch not found → HARD HALT (the user explicitly requested a version that doesn't exist)
- No `--pin` → resolve latest release, fall back to no pin gracefully (user didn't express intent)

This strictness is intentional: `--pin` expresses explicit user intent (analogous to `target_version`), so failure to resolve is an error. Default pin resolution (no `--pin`) is opportunistic, so failure is acceptable.

The shared `skf-validate-pins.py` script handles both the hard validation (deepwiki --pin) and the campaign pin validation (Story 4.4). Campaign will import or invoke the same script.

### Shared Pin Validation Script Interface

The script follows the existing shared script pattern (`skf-preflight.py`, `skf-detect-docs.py`, `skf-shape-detect.py`):

**File:** `src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-pins.py`

```
Args: --repo-url <url> [--pin <version>] [--format tag|branch|any]
Stdout: JSON object (see below)
Exit: 0 (valid/resolved), 1 (invalid — no match), 2 (error)
```

**Output schema:**
```json
{
  "status": "valid | invalid | resolved",
  "pin": "<input_pin_or_null>",
  "resolved_ref": "<matched_tag_or_branch>",
  "ref_type": "tag | branch",
  "version": "<semver_extracted_from_tag_or_null>",
  "suggestions": ["<nearest_tags_on_invalid>"]
}
```

**Behavior matrix:**

| Input | Resolution | Status | Exit |
|-------|-----------|--------|------|
| `--pin v2.0.0` (exists as tag) | Tag match | `valid` | 0 |
| `--pin main` (exists as branch) | Branch match | `valid` | 0 |
| `--pin v99.0.0` (not found) | No match | `invalid` | 1 |
| No `--pin` (latest release exists) | Latest release tag | `resolved` | 0 |
| No `--pin` (no releases) | No tag found | `invalid` | 1 |
| Unreachable repo | Error | stderr JSON | 2 |

**Tag matching priority** (same as `source-resolution-protocols.md`):
1. Exact match: `{pin}`
2. With `v` prefix: `v{pin}`
3. Package scope (monorepos): `{name}@{pin}`, `@{scope}/{name}@{pin}`
4. Crate/package prefix: `{name}/v{pin}`, `{name}/{pin}`, `{name}-v{pin}`

**Version extraction from tag:** Strip `v` prefix if present. If the result is valid semver, set `version`. Otherwise set `version` to null (branch names, non-semver tags).

**Suggestion generation on invalid pin:** List available tags, sort by semver descending, filter to tags sharing a prefix with the pin (e.g., `v99.*` → tags starting with `v99.`). If no prefix matches, return the 5 most recent tags. Cap suggestions at 5.

[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Shared Script Patterns]

### §0b Placement Rationale

§0b (Pin Resolution) sits between §0 (URL Type Detection) and §0c (Coexistence Detection) because:

1. **After §0:** Pin resolution needs to know the input type. Doc URLs skip pinning (no repo to pin against). Local paths get a warning. Only remote repo URLs do full validation.
2. **Before §0c:** Coexistence detection checks existing skills by repo URL/name. Pin doesn't affect which skills match — a pinned `v2.0.0` of `foo` still conflicts with an existing `foo` skill. So §0c runs after pin resolution unchanged.
3. **Before §1:** Shape detection in §1→§3 runs against the source. When a pin is resolved, shape detection should conceptually scope against the pinned ref (though the actual clone/API access happens at CS time — AN's shape detection uses the manifest at HEAD since it doesn't clone the repo).

**Note on shape detection timing:** AN auto-scope does NOT clone the repo — it reads manifests via `gh api` or from a local path. Shape detection at §3 reads the manifest at the default branch (HEAD), not at the pinned ref. This is acceptable because:
- Shape classification (library-API, reference-app, etc.) is structural, not version-specific
- Export counts may differ between versions, but decomposition thresholds are coarse (`>500` exports, `>3` packages) and unlikely to change between versions
- The actual extraction against the pinned ref happens at CS time via `source-resolution-protocols.md`

If a future version requires shape detection at the pinned ref, the solution is to pass `{pinned_ref}` as a `?ref=` parameter to `gh api` calls in §2 — a localized change, not a flow restructure.

### Brief Field Mapping by Ref Type

| Ref Type | `target_version` | `target_ref` | `version` | Effect in CS |
|----------|-----------------|-------------|-----------|-------------|
| Tag (semver) | `{pinned_version}` | `{pinned_ref}` | `{pinned_version}` | Explicit tag resolution → clone at tag |
| Branch | null | `{pinned_ref}` | auto-detect or `1.0.0` | Explicit ref override → clone at branch HEAD |
| No pin, latest resolved | `{pinned_version}` | `{pinned_ref}` | `{pinned_version}` | Same as tag — latest release is a tag |
| No pin, no releases | null | null | auto-detect or `1.0.0` | No pin → implicit resolution or HEAD |

When `target_ref` is set, CS's `source-resolution-protocols.md` uses it verbatim (the "Explicit ref override" path) — `git ls-remote` confirms it resolves, then clones at that ref. When `target_version` is set, the "Explicit Tag Resolution" path runs. Both are existing CS behaviors; Story 2.7 only needs to set the correct brief fields.

[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/source-resolution-protocols.md — "Explicit ref override (when target_ref is set)"]

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

1. **Do NOT modify BS, CS, TS, or EX for pin handling.** The existing brief schema, version resolution, and source resolution protocols already handle `target_version`/`target_ref` natively. Story 2.7 only populates these fields at AN's brief write — no downstream workflow changes needed.

2. **Do NOT clone the repo in §0b.** Pin validation uses `gh api` and `git ls-remote` — lightweight network calls, not full clones. The clone happens at CS time via `source-resolution-protocols.md`.

3. **Do NOT HARD HALT when no releases exist and --pin was not provided.** Default pin resolution (no `--pin`) is opportunistic — if the repo has no release tags, the pipeline continues without pinning (falls back to HEAD, same as today). Only explicit `--pin` triggers a hard gate.

4. **Do NOT put pin validation logic in the step file.** The validation logic goes in `skf-validate-pins.py` (shared module pattern). The step file invokes the script and handles its output. This ensures campaign (Story 4.4) can reuse the same validation.

5. **Do NOT validate pins for doc URLs.** Doc URLs have no git repo, so there's nothing to pin. §0b is skipped entirely for doc URLs.

6. **Do NOT validate pins for local paths via the script.** Local paths can't be validated against remote tags. Instead, emit a warning (same pattern as `source-resolution-protocols.md` → "Local Source Warning") and trust the user to have the correct checkout.

7. **Do NOT renumber existing sections (§0, §0a, §0c, §1-§11).** Insert §0b as a new section without changing the numbering of existing sections.

8. **Do NOT duplicate tag matching logic.** The tag matching priority in `skf-validate-pins.py` must mirror `source-resolution-protocols.md`'s matching rules. If one changes, the other must change too. The shared script is the single source for validation; CS's `source-resolution-protocols.md` uses the brief's `target_ref` verbatim, so it doesn't re-match.

129	
130	    raw = _run_git(["ls-remote", "--tags", repo_url])
131	    if raw:
132	        tags = []
133	        for line in raw.splitlines():
134	            parts = line.split("refs/tags/")
135	            if len(parts) == 2:
136	                tag = parts[1].strip()
137	                if not tag.endswith("^{}"):
138	                    tags.append(tag)
139	        return tags
140	
141	    return []
142	
143	
144	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145	# Branch verification
146	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147	
148	def check_branch_exists(repo_url: str, branch: str) -> bool:
149	    raw = _run_git(["ls-remote", "--heads", repo_url, branch])
150	    if raw and branch in raw:
151	        return True
152	    return False
153	
154	
155	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
156	# Tag matching (mirrors source-resolution-protocols.md)
157	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
158	
159	def _derive_name_from_url(owner: str, repo: str) -> str:
160	    return repo.lower()
161	
162	
163	def match_tag(pin: str, tags: List[str], owner: str, repo: str) -> Optional[str]:
164	    name = _derive_name_from_url(owner, repo)
165	
166	    candidates = [
167	        pin,
168	        f"v{pin}",
169	        f"{name}@{pin}",
170	        f"{name}/v{pin}",
171	        f"{name}/{pin}",
172	        f"{name}-v{pin}",
173	    ]
174	
175	    for candidate in candidates:
176	        if candidate in tags:
177	            return candidate
178	
179	    return None
180	
181	
182	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
183	# Suggestion generation
184	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
185	
186	def _semver_sort_key(tag: str) -> Tuple:
187	    v = extract_version(tag)
188	    if v is None:
189	        return (0, 0, 0, tag)
190	    parts = v.split("-")[0].split(".")
191	    try:
192	        return tuple(int(p) for p in parts[:3])
193	    except ValueError:
194	        return (0, 0, 0, tag)
195	
196	
197	def generate_suggestions(pin: str, tags: List[str], max_count: int = 5) -> List[str]:
198	    if not tags:
199	        return []
200	
201	    prefixes_to_try = []
202	    if "." in pin:
203	        prefixes_to_try.append(pin.rsplit(".", 1)[0] + ".")
204	        prefixes_to_try.append(pin.split(".")[0])
205	    prefixes_to_try.append(pin)
206	
207	    for prefix in prefixes_to_try:
208	        prefix_matches = [t for t in tags if t.startswith(prefix)]
209	        if prefix_matches:
210	            sorted_matches = sorted(prefix_matches, key=_semver_sort_key, reverse=True)
211	            return sorted_matches[:max_count]
212	
213	    sorted_tags = sorted(tags, key=_semver_sort_key, reverse=True)
214	    return sorted_tags[:max_count]
215	
216	
217	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
218	# Latest release resolution
219	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
220	
221	def resolve_latest_release(owner: str, repo: str) -> Optional[str]:
222	    raw = _run_gh([
223	        "api", f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/latest",
224	        "--jq", ".tag_name",
225	    ])
226	    if raw and raw != "null":
227	        return raw.strip()
228	
229	    raw = _run_gh([
230	        "api", f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/tags",
231	        "--jq", ".[0].name",
232	    ])
233	    if raw and raw != "null":
234	        return raw.strip()
235	
236	    return None
237	
238	
239	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
240	# Core validation logic
241	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
242	
243	def validate_pin(
244	    repo_url: str,
245	    pin: Optional[str] = None,
246	    format_filter: str = "any",
247	) -> Dict[str, Any]:
248	    m = _GITHUB_URL_RE.match(repo_url.strip())
249	    if not m:
250	        return {
251	            "status": "invalid",
252	            "pin": pin,
253	            "resolved_ref": None,
254	            "ref_type": None,
255	            "version": None,
256	            "suggestions": [],
257	        }
258	
259	    owner, repo = m.group(1), m.group(2)
260	
261	    if pin is None:
262	        tag = resolve_latest_release(owner, repo)
263	        if tag is None:
264	            return {
265	                "status": "invalid",
266	                "pin": None,
267	                "resolved_ref": None,
268	                "ref_type": None,
269	                "version": None,
270	                "suggestions": [],
271	            }
272	        return {
273	            "status": "resolved",
274	            "pin": None,
275	            "resolved_ref": tag,
276	            "ref_type": "tag",
277	            "version": extract_version(tag),
278	            "suggestions": [],
279	        }
280	
281	    if format_filter in ("tag", "any"):
282	        tags = list_tags(owner, repo, repo_url)
283	        matched = match_tag(pin, tags, owner, repo)
284	        if matched:
285	            return {
286	                "status": "valid",
287	                "pin": pin,
288	                "resolved_ref": matched,
289	                "ref_type": "tag",
290	                "version": extract_version(matched),
291	                "suggestions": [],
292	            }
293	
294	    if format_filter in ("branch", "any"):
295	        if check_branch_exists(repo_url, pin):
296	            return {
297	                "status": "valid",
298	                "pin": pin,
299	                "resolved_ref": pin,
300	                "ref_type": "branch",
301	                "version": None,
302	                "suggestions": [],
303	            }
304	
305	    tags = list_tags(owner, repo, repo_url) if format_filter != "tag" else tags
306	    suggestions = generate_suggestions(pin, tags)
307	
308	    return {
309	        "status": "invalid",
310	        "pin": pin,
311	        "resolved_ref": None,
312	        "ref_type": None,
313	        "version": None,
314	        "suggestions": suggestions,
315	    }
316	
317	
318	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
319	# CLI entry point
320	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
321	
322	def main() -> int:
323	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
324	        description="Validate and resolve version pins for a GitHub repository.",
325	    )
326	    parser.add_argument("--repo-url", required=True, help="GitHub repository URL")
327	    parser.add_argument("--pin", default=None, help="Version pin to validate")
328	    parser.add_argument(
329	        "--format",
330	        choices=["tag", "branch", "any"],
331	        default="any",
332	        help="Restrict matching to tag, branch, or any (default: any)",
333	    )
334	    args = parser.parse_args()
335	
336	    if not _check_gh_available():
337	        json.dump({"error": "gh CLI not found", "code": "GH_NOT_FOUND"}, sys.stderr)
338	        sys.stderr.write("\n")
339	        return 2
340	
341	    m = _GITHUB_URL_RE.match(args.repo_url.strip())
342	    if not m:
343	        json.dump(
344	            {"error": f"Not a GitHub URL: {args.repo_url}", "code": "INVALID_URL"},
345	            sys.stderr,
346	        )
347	        sys.stderr.write("\n")
348	        return 2
349	
350	    try:
351	        result = validate_pin(args.repo_url, args.pin, args.format)
352	    except Exception as exc:
353	        json.dump({"error": str(exc), "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR"}, sys.stderr)
354	        sys.stderr.write("\n")
355	        return 2
356	
357	    json.dump(result, sys.stdout, separators=(",", ":"))
358	    sys.stdout.write("\n")
359	
360	    if result["status"] == "invalid":
361	        return 1
362	    return 0
363	
364	
365	if __name__ == "__main__":
366	    raise SystemExit(main())
367

"""Tests for skf-validate-pins.py.

Pure-function tests for pin validation logic, plus subprocess tests
to verify CLI wiring (argparse, stdout JSON, exit codes).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import importlib.util
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

    Path(__file__).parent.parent
    / "src"
    / "shared"
    / "scripts"
    / "skf-validate-pins.py"
)

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("skf_validate_pins", SCRIPT_PATH)

spec.loader.exec_module(mod)

REPO_URL = "https://github.com/example/repo"


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# assert_result_shape helper
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def assert_result_shape(result: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    assert set(result.keys()) >= {
        "status", "pin", "resolved_ref", "ref_type", "version", "suggestions",
    }, f"Missing keys in output: {result}"
    assert result["status"] in ("valid", "invalid", "resolved")
    assert result["ref_type"] in ("tag", "branch", None)
    assert isinstance(result["suggestions"], list)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Mock helpers
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

SAMPLE_TAGS = [
    "v1.0.0", "v1.1.0", "v1.2.0", "v2.0.0", "v2.0.1",
    "v2.1.0", "v3.0.0-beta.1",
]

SAMPLE_TAGS_WITH_PREFIXES = SAMPLE_TAGS + [
    "repo@1.0.0", "repo/v1.0.0", "repo-v1.0.0",
]


def _make_gh_mock(tags: Optional[List[str]] = None, latest_release: Optional[str] = None):
    """Return a side_effect for subprocess.run that mocks gh/git calls."""
    if tags is None:
        tags = SAMPLE_TAGS

    def side_effect(cmd, **kwargs):
        result = MagicMock()
        if cmd[0] == "gh":
            if cmd[1] == "--version":
                result.returncode = 0
                result.stdout = "gh version 2.0.0"
                return result
            if cmd[1] == "api":
                endpoint = cmd[2]
                if "releases/latest" in endpoint:
                    if latest_release:
                        result.returncode = 0
                        result.stdout = latest_release
                    else:
                        result.returncode = 1
                        result.stdout = ""
                    return result
                if "/tags" in endpoint:
                    result.returncode = 0
                    result.stdout = "\n".join(tags)
                    return result
        if cmd[0] == "git":
            if cmd[1] == "ls-remote":
                if "--heads" in cmd:
                    branch = cmd[-1]
                    if branch == "main":
                        result.returncode = 0
                        result.stdout = f"abc123\trefs/heads/{branch}"
                        return result
                    if branch == "develop":
                        result.returncode = 0
                        result.stdout = f"def456\trefs/heads/{branch}"
                        return result
                    result.returncode = 0
                    result.stdout = ""
                    return result
                if "--tags" in cmd:
                    tag_lines = [f"abc123\trefs/tags/{t}" for t in tags]
                    result.returncode = 0
                    result.stdout = "\n".join(tag_lines)
                    return result
        result.returncode = 1
        result.stdout = ""
        return result
    return side_effect


def _make_unreachable_mock():
    """Mock where all gh/git calls fail."""
    def side_effect(cmd, **kwargs):
        result = MagicMock()
        if cmd[0] == "gh" and cmd[1] == "--version":
            result.returncode = 0
            result.stdout = "gh version 2.0.0"
            return result
        result.returncode = 1
        result.stdout = ""
        result.stderr = "network error"
        return result
    return side_effect


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# extract_version tests
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestExtractVersion:
    def test_semver_tag(self):
        assert mod.extract_version("v2.0.0") == "2.0.0"

    def test_semver_no_prefix(self):
        assert mod.extract_version("1.2.3") == "1.2.3"

    def test_prerelease(self):
        assert mod.extract_version("v3.0.0-beta.1") == "3.0.0-beta.1"

    def test_non_semver(self):
        assert mod.extract_version("main") is None

    def test_non_semver_text(self):
        assert mod.extract_version("release-candidate") is None


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# match_tag tests
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestMatchTag:
    def test_exact_match(self):
        result = mod.match_tag("v2.0.0", SAMPLE_TAGS, "example", "repo")
        assert result == "v2.0.0"

    def test_v_prefix_match(self):
        result = mod.match_tag("2.0.0", SAMPLE_TAGS, "example", "repo")
        assert result == "v2.0.0"

    def test_package_scope_match(self):
        tags = SAMPLE_TAGS + ["repo@2.0.0"]
        result = mod.match_tag("2.0.0", tags, "example", "repo")
        assert result == "v2.0.0"

    def test_package_scope_only(self):
        tags = ["repo@2.0.0"]
        result = mod.match_tag("2.0.0", tags, "example", "repo")
        assert result == "repo@2.0.0"

    def test_crate_prefix_slash_v(self):
        tags = ["repo/v2.0.0"]
        result = mod.match_tag("2.0.0", tags, "example", "repo")
        assert result == "repo/v2.0.0"

    def test_crate_prefix_slash(self):
        tags = ["repo/2.0.0"]
        result = mod.match_tag("2.0.0", tags, "example", "repo")
        assert result == "repo/2.0.0"

    def test_crate_prefix_dash_v(self):
        tags = ["repo-v2.0.0"]
        result = mod.match_tag("2.0.0", tags, "example", "repo")
        assert result == "repo-v2.0.0"

    def test_no_match(self):
        result = mod.match_tag("v99.0.0", SAMPLE_TAGS, "example", "repo")
        assert result is None


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# generate_suggestions tests
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestGenerateSuggestions:
    def test_prefix_match(self):
        suggestions = mod.generate_suggestions("v2", SAMPLE_TAGS)
        assert len(suggestions) <= 5
        assert all(s.startswith("v2") for s in suggestions)

    def test_no_prefix_match_returns_recent(self):
        suggestions = mod.generate_suggestions("v99", SAMPLE_TAGS)
        assert len(suggestions) <= 5
        assert len(suggestions) > 0

    def test_empty_tags(self):
        suggestions = mod.generate_suggestions("v1.0.0", [])
        assert suggestions == []

    def test_max_count(self):
        many_tags = [f"v1.0.{i}" for i in range(20)]
        suggestions = mod.generate_suggestions("v1", many_tags, max_count=5)
        assert len(suggestions) == 5


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# validate_pin tests — valid semver tag
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestValidSemverTag:
    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_valid_tag(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock()
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin="v2.0.0")
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "valid"
        assert result["resolved_ref"] == "v2.0.0"
        assert result["ref_type"] == "tag"
        assert result["version"] == "2.0.0"

    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_valid_v_prefixed_tag(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock()
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin="2.0.0")
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "valid"
        assert result["resolved_ref"] == "v2.0.0"
        assert result["ref_type"] == "tag"
        assert result["version"] == "2.0.0"


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# validate_pin tests — valid branch name
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestValidBranch:
    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_valid_branch(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock()
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin="main")
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "valid"
        assert result["resolved_ref"] == "main"
        assert result["ref_type"] == "branch"
        assert result["version"] is None


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# validate_pin tests — invalid pin with suggestions
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestInvalidPin:
    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_invalid_pin(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock()
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin="v99.0.0")
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "invalid"
        assert result["resolved_ref"] is None
        assert len(result["suggestions"]) > 0

    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_invalid_pin_with_prefix_suggestions(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock()
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin="v2.9.9")
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "invalid"
        assert any(s.startswith("v2") for s in result["suggestions"])


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# validate_pin tests — latest release resolution (no --pin)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestLatestRelease:
    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_latest_release(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock(latest_release="v3.0.0")
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin=None)
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "resolved"
        assert result["resolved_ref"] == "v3.0.0"
        assert result["ref_type"] == "tag"
        assert result["version"] == "3.0.0"
        assert result["pin"] is None

    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_no_releases_fallback_to_tags(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock(tags=["v1.0.0"], latest_release=None)
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin=None)
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "resolved"
        assert result["resolved_ref"] == "v1.0.0"

    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_no_releases_no_tags(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock(tags=[], latest_release=None)
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin=None)
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "invalid"
        assert result["pin"] is None


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# validate_pin tests — error handling (unreachable repo)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestErrorHandling:
    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_unreachable_repo(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_unreachable_mock()
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin="v1.0.0")
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "invalid"

    def test_invalid_url(self):
        result = mod.validate_pin("not-a-github-url", pin="v1.0.0")
        assert_result_shape(result)
        assert result["status"] == "invalid"


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# validate_pin tests — format filter
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestFormatFilter:
    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_tag_only(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock()
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin="main", format_filter="tag")
        assert result["status"] == "invalid"

    @patch("subprocess.run")
    def test_branch_only(self, mock_run):
        mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock()
        result = mod.validate_pin(REPO_URL, pin="v2.0.0", format_filter="branch")
        assert result["status"] == "invalid"


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLI subprocess tests
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestCLI:
    def test_cli_valid_pin(self):
        with patch("subprocess.run") as mock_run:
            mock_run.side_effect = _make_gh_mock()
            result = subprocess.run(
                [sys.executable, str(SCRIPT_PATH), "--repo-url", REPO_URL, "--pin", "v2.0.0"],
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
            )
        if result.returncode == 0:
            output = json.loads(result.stdout)
            assert_result_shape(output)

    def test_cli_missing_repo_url(self):
        result = subprocess.run(
            [sys.executable, str(SCRIPT_PATH)],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )
        assert result.returncode != 0

1	---
2	name: skf-analyze-source
3	description: Discover what to skill in a large repo and produce recommended skill briefs. Use when the user requests to "analyze source for skills" or "discover skill opportunities."
4	---
5	
6	# Analyze Source
7	
8	## Overview
9	
10	Analyzes a large repo or multi-service project to identify discrete skillable units, map exports and integration points, and produce recommended skill-brief.yaml files as the primary entry point for brownfield onboarding. The analysis must be thorough enough to produce actionable briefs, but scoped enough to avoid overwhelming the user with false positives. Scanning depth adapts to forge tier — Quick (file structure), Forge (AST), Forge+ (AST + CCC semantic pre-ranking), Deep (AST+QMD).
11	
12	## Conventions
13	
14	- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
15	- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
16	- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
17	- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
18	- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.
19	
20	## Role
21	
22	You are a source code analyst and decomposition architect collaborating with a developer onboarding an existing project. You bring expertise in codebase analysis, service boundary detection, and skill scoping, while the user brings their domain knowledge. Work together as equals.
23	
24	## Workflow Rules
25	
26	These rules apply to every step in this workflow:
27	
28	- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
29	- Always communicate in `{communication_language}` (the language for user-facing prose). Written artifact text — the per-unit recommendation `description` and `scope.notes` persisted into `skill-brief.yaml` — is in `{document_output_language}`; per-step rules call this out where it applies. The two values may be the same.
30	- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision
31	
32	## Stages
33	
34	| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed | Condition |
35	|---|------|------|--------------|-----------|
36	| 1 | Initialize | references/init.md | Yes | Always |
37	| 1a | Auto-Scope | references/step-auto-scope.md | Yes | `[auto]` mode only. Includes §0b pin resolution — validates and resolves version pin before scoping. Includes §0c coexistence detection — checks for existing skills before proceeding. Includes §0 docs-only URL detection — doc URLs short-circuit auto-scope entirely |
38	| 1b | Continue (session resume) | references/continue.md | Yes | Always |
39	| 2 | Scan Project | references/scan-project.md | No (confirm) | Interactive mode only |
40	| 3 | Identify Units | references/identify-units.md | No (confirm) | Interactive mode only |
41	| 4 | Map & Detect | references/map-and-detect.md | Yes | Interactive mode only |
42	| 5 | Recommend | references/recommend.md | No (confirm) | Interactive mode only |
43	| 6 | Generate Briefs | references/generate-briefs.md | Yes | Interactive mode only |
44	| 7 | Workflow Health Check | references/health-check.md | Yes | Always |
45	
46	**Auto mode path:** When `[auto]` flag is present, init (step 1) routes directly to step 1a, which performs manifest scan → shape detection → scope generation → brief write → health check, bypassing steps 2–6. After URL type detection (§0), pin resolution (§0b) validates the `--pin` argument (or resolves the latest release tag) and stores the resolved ref for downstream brief writes. Coexistence detection (§0c) then checks for existing skills matching the target. If found, the user chooses alongside/merge/skip. Headless mode auto-selects alongside. Auto-scope may produce N > 1 confirmed units when decomposition thresholds are met (`export_count > 500` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` or `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]`), resulting in N briefs and N `brief_paths` in the envelope. When the target is a documentation URL (not a GitHub repo or local path), auto-scope detects the docs-only input at §0, validates URL reachability, writes a docs-only brief, and emits the envelope without performing source analysis.
47	
48	**Shape detection reference:** `references/step-shape-detect.md` — loaded by step 1a as a reference doc (not a chained step).
49	
50	## Invocation Contract
51	
52	| Aspect | Detail |
53	|--------|--------|
54	| **Inputs** | project_path [required], scope_hint [optional]. `project_path` can be a GitHub repo URL, a local filesystem path, or a documentation URL for docs-only mode. The URL type heuristic at §0 determines the mode. |
55	| **Headless inputs** | `--project-path <path>` (skip Step 1 project-path prompt; accepts documentation URLs for docs-only mode), `--scope-hint <text>` (skip Step 1 scope-hint prompt), `--intent-hint <text>` (pre-supply analysis intent; drives recommendation ranking in Step 5), `--pin <version>` (pin to a specific version tag or branch; accepts semver tags, git tags, and branch names; when absent, resolves to the latest release tag) |
56	| **Headless flag** | `--headless` / `-H` flips every confirm gate to auto-proceed |
57	| **Auto flag** | `[auto]` bracket modifier — activates auto-scope mode (step 1a). Pipelines pass this as `AN[auto]`. When active, init routes to `step-auto-scope.md` which performs shape detection → scope generation → brief write, bypassing interactive steps 2–6. Requires `--project-path`. |
58	| **Gates** | step 2: Confirm Gate [C] | step 3: Confirm Gate [C] | step 5: Confirm Gate [C] (all skipped in auto mode) |
59	| **Outputs** | analysis-report.md, skill-brief.yaml files (one per recommended unit); final `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` line on stdout when `{headless_mode}` is true. In auto mode, the envelope includes `"mode":"auto"`. |
60	| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with default action when `{headless_mode}` is true |
61	| **Exit codes** | See "Exit Codes" below |
62	
63	## Exit Codes
64	
65	Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable code so headless automators can branch on the failure class without grepping message text:
66	
67	| Code | Meaning              | Raised by                                                                                  |
68	| ---- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
69	| 0    | success / skipped / redirect | step 7 (terminal — health check completion); also covers coexistence `"skipped"` and `"redirect"` statuses from §0c |
70	| 2    | input-missing        | step 1 §2-3 — required config absent (config.yaml not loadable, project path empty/invalid in headless mode); step 1 §2b — auto mode without `--project-path` |
71	| 3    | resolution-failure   | step 1 §2 (`forge-tier.yaml` missing at `{sidecar_path}/forge-tier.yaml`); step 1 §3 (project path does not exist or remote URL inaccessible); step 1a §0a (docs-only URL unreachable); step 1a §0b (pin validation failure — `halt_reason: "pin-invalid"` when the supplied `--pin` does not match any tag or branch); step 1a §3 (shape detection script error, exit code 2) |
72	| 4    | write-failure        | step 1 §6 (analysis report write failed); step 6 §5 (skill-brief.yaml write failed); step 6 §9 (result contract write failed) |
73	| 6    | user-cancelled       | any interactive menu in steps 2/3/5/6 (user selected `[X]` Cancel and exit)               |
74	
75	## Result Contract (Headless)
76	
77	When `{headless_mode}` is true, step 6 (interactive) or step 1a (auto) emits a single-line JSON envelope on **stdout** before chaining to step 7, and every HARD HALT emits the same envelope shape on **stderr** with `status: "error"`:
78	
79	```
80	SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success|error","report_path":"…|null","brief_paths":["…"],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":N,"skipped":N,"maybe":N},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"interactive|auto"}
81	```
82	
83	`status` is `"success"` on the terminal happy path, `"error"` on any HALT, `"redirect"` when coexistence detection routes to US (merge), or `"skipped"` when the user skips a conflicting target. `halt_reason` is one of: `null` (success), `"input-missing"`, `"forge-tier-missing"`, `"path-invalid"`, `"pin-invalid"`, `"write-failed"`, `"user-cancelled"`. `exit_code` matches the table above. `brief_paths` is an array of absolute paths to every generated `skill-brief.yaml` (empty array if none were generated). `unit_counts` reports confirmed/skipped/maybe counts from step 5's user decisions. `mode` is `"auto"` when the `[auto]` flag was active, `"interactive"` otherwise (omitting `mode` is equivalent to `"interactive"` for backward compatibility). The `coexistence` field (present when §0c triggers) is `"alongside"`, `"merge"`, or `"skip"`, indicating the user's coexistence decision. The `pinned_ref` and `pinned_version` fields (present when §0b resolves a pin) record the resolved git ref and extracted semver version for provenance tracking.
84	
85	## On Activation
86	
87	1. Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and resolve:
88	   - `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`, `forge_data_folder`, `skills_output_folder`, `sidecar_path`
89	
90	2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in preferences.yaml. Default: false.
91	
92	3. **Resolve workflow customization.** Run:
93	
94	   ```bash
95	   python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py \
96	       --skill {skill-root} --key workflow
97	   ```
98	
99	   The script merges the three customization layers per `bmad-customize`'s structural merge rules (scalars override, arrays append):
100	
101	   - `{skill-root}/customize.toml` — bundled defaults
102	   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.toml` under `{project-root}` — team overrides (committed)
103	   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.user.toml` under `{project-root}` — personal overrides (gitignored)
104	
105	   If the script fails or is missing, fall back to reading `{skill-root}/customize.toml` directly — the bundled defaults are an empty string for each path scalar.
106	
107	   Apply the path-scalar fallback now so stage files don't have to repeat the conditional logic. For each scalar, if the merged value is empty or absent, use the bundled default:
108	
109	   - `{unitDetectionHeuristicsPath}` ← `workflow.unit_detection_heuristics_path` if non-empty, else `references/unit-detection-heuristics.md`
110	   - `{briefSchemaPath}` ← `workflow.brief_schema_path` if non-empty, else `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`
111	   - `{analysisReportTemplatePath}` ← `workflow.analysis_report_template_path` if non-empty, else `templates/analysis-report-template.md`
112	   - `{onCompleteCommand}` ← `workflow.on_complete` if non-empty, else empty string (no-op — workflow skips the hook invocation)
113	
114	   Stash all four as workflow-context variables. Stage files reference `{unitDetectionHeuristicsPath}` / `{briefSchemaPath}` / `{analysisReportTemplatePath}` / `{onCompleteCommand}` directly — no conditional at the usage site. Empty-string overrides cleanly fall through to the bundled default; non-empty values let orgs swap in house-style copies (or wire in pipeline hooks) without forking the skill.
115	
116	4. Load, read the full file, and then execute `references/init.md` to begin the workflow.
117
